title: hydrangea scandens subsp. kwangtungensis
intro: Hydrangea scandens subsp. kwangtungensis, also known as Kwangtung hydrangea or Chinese hydrangea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Hydrangeaceae. It is a deciduous shrub native to southern China, valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and are believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, offering benefits such as improved cardiovascular health and reduced risk of chronic diseases. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction, tea, or infusion, and is also used in herbal preparations, including capsules and tinctures.
